metadata.title: States PCS: Punjab GK Questions for PPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which city was Punjab’s 1980s SSI hub for automobile components?
questions.explanation: Ludhiana became Punjab’s automobile parts SSI hub in 1980s, supplying OEMs/replacement markets. It also boasts the world’s second-largest bicycle and parts manufacturing. Other cities like Jalandhar, Kapurthala, and Patiala contributed, but Ludhiana was dominant in the auto-component sector.

questions.question: Which city in Punjab is Asia’s largest bicycle manufacturing hub?
questions.explanation: Ludhiana leads Asia in bicycle manufacturing, housing major units like Hero Cycles and several small-scale factories. It contributes significantly to Punjab’s industrial output and is globally recognized for its cycle and cycle parts industry.

questions.question: What is the installed power capacity of Punjab in megawatts (MW)?
questions.explanation: Punjab’s installed power capacity: 4967 MW (thermal + hydro). Highest per capita energy use (2017-18): industry 16,000 MkWh, domestic 13,796 MkWh, agriculture 12,356 MkWh. Agriculture and industry rely heavily on energy for growth. Renewable resources are sustainable; non-renewable deplete.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of Northern Railway zone situated?
questions.explanation: Northern Railway zone was established in 1952 with its headquarters at Delhi. It is one of the 19 zones of Indian Railways, covering major areas of North India, and is the largest railway zone in India by route kilometers.

questions.question: Who was Punjab’s first and only woman Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: Rajinder Kaur Bhattal became Punjab’s first and only female Chief Minister in 1996. She was the 8th woman Chief Minister in India and belongs to the Indian National Congress. Her tenure marked a rare instance of female leadership in Punjab politics.

questions.question: Who wrote the first Punjabi play, Raja Lakhdata Singh?
questions.explanation: Bhai Vir Singh authored Raja Lakhdata Singh (1910), recognized as the first Punjabi play. He was a pioneer of modern Punjabi literature, won the first Sahitya Akademi Award in Punjabi (1955), and was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1956.

questions.question: Which grass is mainly used in Punjab’s traditional basketry?
questions.explanation: Sarkanda grass is widely used in Punjab for basketry, mat-making, and hand fans, owing to its flexibility and availability. It is an essential raw material for Punjabi craft items, notably Peshawari Pankkha and household goods, reflecting traditional rural craftsmanship of the state.

questions.question: Which Punjab city houses the historic Qila Mubarak Androon?
questions.explanation: Qila Mubarak Androon, a significant historical fort, is located in Bathinda, Punjab. Dating back to the 6th century CE, it is associated with the imprisonment of Razia Sultana, India’s first female monarch, and remains a prominent tourist and heritage site in the region.

questions.question: In Punjab’s Phulkari, which month is favored in Baramah songs of separation?
questions.explanation: Phulkari embroidery is intertwined with Punjab’s folk songs. Baramah songs express emotions of seasonal separation and often highlight the month of Sawan, associated with rain and longing. Sawan holds importance in Punjabi literature, folk traditions, and is especially cited in emotional Baramah compositions.

questions.question: Which bowed instrument with deep tone is linked to Guru Arjan Dev Ji?
questions.explanation: Saranda is a bowed string instrument known for its deep tone and is associated with Guru Arjan Dev Ji. Unlike the Taus and Dilruba, it is compact. Sarangi and Sapp are used in Punjabi music and dance but are not linked to Guru Arjan Dev Ji.
